characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: His parents call him "Bobby". Alliterative Name:Bob theBuilder. All-Loving Hero: He's very kind and loving to everyone around him, even his machines, whom he cares for greatly. Broken Tears: During the Christmas special. After a long day of doing chores for the mayor, Bob finds out his brother Tom *won’t* be visiting him for Christmas and has a moment of genuine sadness. Catchphrase:“Right team, can we fix it?” in the stop-motion series.In the 2015 reboot, this is altered to “Can we build it?”.“Morning, team!” in Ready, Steady, Build!.

characters.name: Bob the Builder
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: His parents call him "Bobby". Alliterative Name:Bob theBuilder. All-Loving Hero: He's very kind and loving to everyone around him, even his machines, whom he cares for greatly. Broken Tears: During the Christmas special. After a long day of doing chores for the mayor, Bob finds out his brother Tom *won’t* be visiting him for Christmas and has a moment of genuine sadness. Catchphrase:“Right team, can we fix it?” in the stop-motion series.In the 2015 reboot, this is altered to “Can we build it?”.“Morning, team!” in Ready, Steady, Build!.

characters.name: Wendy
characters.description: Catchphrase: “Okay team, you heard Bob: let's get to work!” in the reboot. Characterization Marches On: Wendy wasn't a builder originally, acting solely as Bob's secretary. Starting in the second series, she was shown doing construction work with Bob and the machines, with her secretary role being discarded as the season went on. Costume Evolution: She wore a green checkered suit jacket, a short-sleeved light blue shirt, white scarf and red skirt in the first series and early in the second, reflecting her initial secretary role. In "Lofty to the Rescue" she trades in her skirt for blue jeans and midway through Series 2 discards the jacket and scarf as she becomes a builder, with her shirt being long-sleeved starting in "Tea Set Travis" (which has her old jacket being recycled). From Series 3 on her shirt becomes green and she only wears her formal outfit on days off or special occasions until it's phased out in Series 6. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: She has blonde hair and is very loving. Image Song:"Blonde Haired Gal in a Hard Hat"for the classic series and for Project: Build It,"Hey Wendy"and“Picking Up The Pieces”. Ready, Steady, Build! made a short US dubnotehttps://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u18K_eYL3hQof the latter song.

characters.name: Scoop
characters.description: Adaptational Dumbass: His personality in the 2015 reboot counts as this as he became more child-like compared to his original persona. Bad Liar: Given that he's normally very honest with Bob, he struggles to do the contrary. Big Brother Mentor: Becomes this to Benny in the "Project: Build It" era. Catchphrase:“I can dig it!” “Can we [verb] it?” and “No prob, Bob!” in the original.“Oh, I’ve just had a BRILLIANT IDEA!” in the reboot. “I can dig it!” “Can we [verb] it?” and “No prob, Bob!” in the original.

characters.name: Muck
characters.description: Adaptational Dumbass: In the 2015 reboot, he's much moreclueless and careless. The Big Guy: By far the strongest of Bob's machines, and useful for hauling materials. Catchphrase:“Let’s get mucky!” and “Muck to the rescue!” in the original.“Oh, wow! I’d love to join in!” in the reboot (UK only). “Let’s get mucky!” and “Muck to the rescue!” in the original. “Oh, wow! I’d love to join in!” in the reboot (UK only).

characters.name: Lofty
characters.description: Adaptational Personality Change: In the 2015 reboot, he is much more confident, sarcastic, and somewhat bossy. Blue Is Heroic: Painted blue, and has saved the day on multiple occasions. Catchphrase:"Uh, yeah, I think so!" in the original."It's a good job done." in the reboot. "Uh, yeah, I think so!" in the original. "It's a good job done." in the reboot.

characters.name: Roley
characters.description: Adaptational Personality Change: In the 2015 reboot, he is a huge perfectionist. Catchphrase:"Rock and roll!" in the original.“The tarmac is flat!” in the reboot. "Rock and roll!" in the original. “The tarmac is flat!” in the reboot. Characterization Marches On: In the early episodes, Roley was more chilled and easy-going.

characters.name: Dizzy
characters.description: The Baby of the Bunch: She's the youngest of the Can-Do Crew. Catchphrase:“I can mix it!” and “Brilliant!” in the original.“Pour to perfection!” in the reboot. “I can mix it!” and “Brilliant!” in the original. “Pour to perfection!” in the reboot. Commonality Connection: She and Roley are good friends because they both like music.

characters.name: Pilchard
characters.description: Action Pet: On occasion, goes above and beyond the responsibilities of a simple house cat. A notable example is during “Pilchard’s Pets,” when she helps herd all of the freed animals in the new petting zoo. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Pilchard is a blue cat. Amplified Animal Aptitude: With a few notable exceptions, she is usually able to communicate her needs and emotions with Bob and the crew. Cats Are Mean: Downplayed, Pilchard can sometimes get up to mischief, but she never deliberately tries to interfere with Bob & the team. Image Song:Find That Cat

characters.name: Farmer Percy Pickles
characters.description: Alliterative Name:PercyPickles. Deep South: Farmer Pickles speaks with a southern accent in the US dub ofProject: Build ItandReady, Steady, Build!. Hidden Depths: Used to play in an Oompa band. Image Song:"Down on the Farm"notehttps://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UVaswiW8yNEfor the US versionwith Spud. Only Sane Man: Often fills this role when Bob and Wendy aren’t around, giving advice and instruction to the machines and Spud.

characters.name: Mrs. Potts
characters.description: No Full Name Given: We never do find out her first name. Put on a Bus: She stops appearing afterWendy's Party Plan. Terrible Artist: Not an artist herself, but her taste in lawn ornaments is often laughed at by Bob and his crew. Unusual Pets for Unusual People: in addition to a rarely-mentioned pet dog, there’s an episode revolving around Mrs Potts’ pet turtle Tommy, and she later adopts a family birds.

characters.name: Mrs. Broadbent
characters.description: No Full Name Given: We just know her last name. Out of Focus: Arguably the least prominent reoccurring member of Bobsville, having only two episodes that give her a major role in the entire main series. Reused Character Design: Her hairstyle is similar to Mrs. Potts.

characters.name: Mrs. Percival
characters.description: Dance Enthusiast: As seen when she teaches Bob to Tango, she’s a passionate dancer. Jack of All Trades: As a teacher, she’s one of the more cultured and talented members of Bobsville. Various episodes have shown her hosting plays and fundraisers, teaching Bob how to dance, and even running a dog show. No Full Name Given: We only ever learn her last name. Reused Character Design: Her hairstyle is similar to both Mrs. Broadbent and Mrs. Potts.

characters.name: Mr. Bernard Bentley
characters.description: Adaptational Dumbass: Big time in the 2015 reboot, where he's reduced to a bumbling buffoon whenever he's around Mayor Madison. Alliterative Name:BernardBentley. Benevolent Boss: Or rather Benevolent Inspector. While Mr. Bentley makes sure that a job is being done to the correct standards, he is not, at all stingy. In fact, he is actually a firm but fair man. Bumbling Sidekick: He is this to Mayor Madison in the 2015 reboot. Catchphrase:“Oh, my word!” in the original.“Oh, dear me! Mayor Madison will be here any second!” in the reboot.

characters.name: Mr. Dixon
characters.description: Nice Guy: One of the more light-hearted members of the community, almost always seen making jokes and laughing or giving compliments. No Full Name Given: He’s just “Mr. Dixon”. Unusual Pets for Unusual People: Not unusual, but absolutely appropriate for his line of work—he has two pet carrier pigeons.

characters.name: Mr. Jeremy Ellis
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: After vanishing at the start of theProject: Build Itera, he makes one final return in “Scrambler And The Colourful Cave”. Stage Magician: At least two episodes show he is a talented magician. Unusual Pets for Unusual People: In line with his hobby as a magician, he has two pet doves.

characters.name: Lennie Lazenby
characters.description: Alliterative Name:LennieLazenby. Artist and the Band: His band is named “Lennie And The Lazers”. Big Damn Heroes: Serves as this in "A Christmas to Remember" by flying his private jet to Tom's residence in the Artic Circle when the latter misses the boat to the mainland. Dance Party Ending: He and his guitar are responsible for the one in “Roley And The Rock Star”. Lost Voice Plot: In “A Christmas To Remember” he ends up losing his voice on the day his band is supposed to perform their Christmas concert.

characters.name: Mr. Beasley
characters.description: Chekhov's Hobby: Reveals he collects pressed flowers right after Roley runs over his flowerbed. The Ditherer: His first main personality trait. D.I.Y. Disaster: Any project he attempts himself will become this, despite him thinking otherwise. Early-Bird Cameo: His name is offhandedly mentioned in “Runaway Roley”, but he doesn’t actually appear until “Forget-Me-Knot Bob”. The Klutz: He’s not themostuncoordinated person out there, but this is still his second main trait.

characters.name: Bunty Ferguson
characters.description: Accidental Misnaming: Can never seem to get Mr Bentley’s name right. The Bus Came Back: After her debut, she doesn’t appear again until “Bob’s Big Plan”. Mean Boss: Repeatedly calls Mr. Bentley by the wrong name, makes unreasonable demands of Bob regarding the town’s Christmas decorations, criticizes him for having a brief sip of tea, and disregards his need to finish Christmas shopping. Unseen No More: Is only ever mentioned, never seen until “A Christmas to Remember.” Uptight Loves Wild: Seemingly has a thing for Banger.

characters.name: Tom
characters.description: Action Hero: Established in his scene saving a reindeer that had fallen off of an icy cliff. Dudley Do-Right Stops to Help: The reason he misses the boat to the Mainland is because he stopped to help a deer reunite with its father. Friend to All Living Things: Befitting a zoologist, he's very protective towards all animals. No Full Name Given: Although presumably his last name is the same as Bob’s. No Good Deed Goes Unpunished: He misses the ship to see his brother because he stopped to help an imperiled reindeer.

characters.name: John
characters.description: No Celebrities Were Harmed: He’s Elton John in everything but (first) name; heck, he’s even voiced by him! One-Shot Character: Only appears in “A Christmas To Remember”

characters.name: Banger
characters.description: Innocently Insensitive: Banger can be *very* casual in proper settings, calling Mr Bentley mate and patting him on the head during conversation. Only Known by Their Nickname: Everyone calls him Banger. Running Gag: He spends most of the special he debuts in dropping things and then exclaiming, “No damage!” The Reliable One: Surprisingly. Despite his laid back and casual demeanor, Banger is a team player all-around and helps Bob and Mr Bentley to ensure everything is ready for the town’s Christmas celebration.

characters.name: Mr. Angelo Sabatini
characters.description: Catchphrase: Has a few, including “That’s fantastico!” “Bellissimo!” "Can-a we fix it?" and, of course, “Mamma-mia!” Freeze-Frame Bonus: Has a picture of himself with Mr Dixon’s brother, the famous soccer player David Dixon hanging in his restaurant. Gratuitous Italian: “Bellissimo!” Local Hangout: Somewhat. While it’s not explicitly a hang-out spot, it is the only named restaurant in Bobsville. Hidden Depths: He's revealed to be able to play the violin in "Roley's Important Job", and is also a talented soccer player.

characters.name: Jenny
characters.description: No Full Name Given: She’s just “Jenny”. Tomboy and Girly Girl: The Girly Girl to her sister Wendy's Tomboy.

characters.name: Mr. Adam Fothergill
characters.description: Early-Bird Cameo: He appears at least three times, in episodes that go all the way back to Season 3, before he properly, actually debuts. Given Name Reveal: Mr. Fothergill is not named until he is properly introduced inClumsy Roley. Plot Allergy: His mysterious illness in “Hamish’s New Home” turns out to be the result of him being allergic to Hamish, his pet parrot. This leads to it being adopted by J.J. and Molly. Sickly Neurotic Geek: Only when it pertains to his allergies—he’s almost always portrayed as congested and struggling. The Smart Guy: Downplayed, but his profession is helping people with their computers, so he is tech savvy.

characters.name: Doris Ellis
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Aside from cameoing in “A Christmas To Remember”, that is.

characters.name: Mrs. Barbara Bentley
characters.description: Behind Every Great Man: As seen in his home life, Mrs Bentley is often seen keeping her husband’s life in order.

characters.name: J.J.
characters.description: Geek: Not as much as Mr. Bentley, but he can get pretty passionate about the specifics of his work, and enjoys flying his model plane. Mirror Character: A minor example. He’s a counterpart to Bob, he’s the one who keeps the rest of his team in his yard on track. Not So Above It All: He’s initially against adopting Hamish the parrot, but quickly changes his attitude and fawns over it by the end of the episode. Only Known by Their Nickname: We never get any indication for what, if anything, “J.J.” stands for. Team Dad: In the literal sense for Molly, but Skip and Trix also regard him this way.

characters.name: Molly
characters.description: Hidden Depths: She’s talented at designing clothes, and takes a class to learn first aid. Mirror Character: A counterpart to Wendy, and one of her closest friends besides Bob. Missing Mom: Her mother is never seen or mentioned. No Full Name Given: As a knock-on effect of the audience not knowing J.J.’s name. Token Minority: Just like her father.

characters.name: David Dixon
characters.description: Alliterative Name:DavidDixon. Freeze-Frame Bonus: There’s a picture of him hanging in Mr Sabatini’s restaurant. No Celebrities Were Harmed: Based on the famous football (soccer) player David Beckham. Remember the New Guy?: He’s the series’ second case of an existing character suddenly manifesting a brother out of the ether.

characters.name: Robert and Dot
characters.description: Ancestral Name: Robert was the original Bob- in fact, it’s him that the town of Bobsville is named after. Cool Old Guy: Even after retiring, Robert still enjoyed being a builder, and despite his age, he's still active enough to join his "Bobby" on building jobs. Everyone Calls Him "Barkeep": The machines refer to them as "Bob's Mum and Dad". Strong Family Resemblance: Robert looks like Bob and Tom but with gray hair.

characters.name: Dr. Florence Mountfitchet
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Technically, she does cameo in “Bob’s Big Plan”, but she only has a proper role in “The Knights Of Can-A-Lot”.

characters.name: Mr. Steven
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Racing Muck”.

characters.name: Tony
characters.description: One-Steve Limit: Violates this with Tony Toberomi.

characters.name: Pam Goody
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: Aside from some random background cameos, she’s absent from her debut episode to “Mr. Bentley’s Winter Fair”.

characters.name: Katie
characters.description: Bringing in the Expert: She’s a specialist in a field of work that even Bob can’t do. No Full Name Given: She’s just “Katie”. One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Spud And The Doves”

characters.name: Mrs. Sophia Sabatini
characters.description: Alliterative Name:SophiaSabatini. Gratuitous Italian: Just like her husband.

characters.name: Mr. Costello
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Trix And The Bug”

characters.name: Jana Von Strudel
characters.description: Funny Foreigner: She has an Austrian accent and yodels, and her last name is a kind of German pastry. Yodel Land: Apparently, this is where she comes from.

characters.name: Travis
characters.description: Alliterative Name:TravisTractor. Catchphrase: Has a few.“Travis Tractor’s my name, moving loads is my game.” (Or sometimes, “packing loads is my game.”)“I'd better get going!”“Don't be silly, Spud!”“Okay, Farmer Pickles!” “Travis Tractor’s my name, moving loads is my game.” (Or sometimes, “packing loads is my game.”) “I'd better get going!” “Don't be silly, Spud!”

characters.name: Scoot
characters.description: Alliterative Name: Well, nickname, but she likes to call herself “Speedster Scoot”. Catchphrase: “Speedster Scoot hits the track! Woo-hoo!” Meaningful Name: She’s named for her ability to scoot around on snow. One-Shot Character: Only appears in “A Christmas To Remember”.

characters.name: Skip
characters.description: A Lizard Named "Liz": A skip carrier named Skip. Dub Name Change: Becomes “Conti” in the German dub. Mirror Character: A counterpart to Lofty, with a bit of Roley’s laxidazicalness.

characters.name: Trix
characters.description: Catchphrase: “Okey-dokey, J.J.!” Dub Name Change: Becomes “Wózek” in Polish, “Kessi” in German, and “La Vadora” in Catalan. Mirror Character: A counterpart to Dizzy and often her partner in trouble, with hints of Muck’s recklessness.

characters.name: Benny
characters.description: Catchphrase: "Unreal, banana peel!" Cousin Oliver:Kid-Appeal Character? Check. Didn’t join up with the cast until quite a ways into the show? Check. Kid-Appeal Character: Very cheerful, childish, and young compared to the Can-Do Crew, reflecting the target audience.

characters.name: Zoomer
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: After the special he debuts in, he doesn’t appear again until thesixteenthseason. Catchphrase: “Zoom, zoom, zoom!” Meaningful Name: Refers to his speed on snow. Reused Character Design: Almost literally; his model is just Scoot’s with a new coat of paint.

characters.name: Spud
characters.description: Animal Lover: Despite being a scarecrow, Spud actually gets along with most birds and other animals. He’s friends with Pilchard and Scruffty, briefly acts as a mother hen to a freshly-hatched chick, and even enjoys birdwatching. About the only animals he *doesn’t* get along with are crows and Humpty. Animal Nemesis: Has this relationship with Squawk and, in early seasons, Bird. Animal Sweet on Object: In “A Christmas To Remember”, he comes across a (completely normal and inanimate) Christmas angel decoration which he promptly decides is the most beautiful “woman” he’s ever seen, and dances a rather one-sided tango with her. Animate Inanimate Object: He’s a walking, talking scarecrow. Big Eater: Best shown when he overeats apples in "Naughty Spud".

characters.name: Bird
characters.description: A Dog Named "Dog": He's a bird named Bird. Advertised Extra: He still appears and is mentioned in the intro to "Project: Build It", despite barely appearing anymore. Amplified Animal Aptitude: Can communicate fluently with Roley, and with Spud and the machines to varying degrees. Cartoon Creature: He does not appear to be based on any real-life bird species. Demoted to Extra: He barely appears in the later seasons of the original series, as well asProject: Build ItandReady, Steady, Build!

characters.name: Scruffty
characters.description: Action Pet: Is often seen performing his duties as a watchdog, sheep-herder, and protector to the other farm animals. Friendly Enemy: Sometimes nice to Pilchard, sometimes antagonistic. Hoist by His Own Petard: In one episode Scruffty digs a hole so deep that he falls into a rabbit den and needs rescuing. Predator-Prey Friendship: In later seasons is seen getting along with Pilchard.

characters.name: Squawk
characters.description: A Dog Named "Dog": Squawk is the sound a crow makes. Amplified Animal Aptitude: Can communicate with Spud and the machines to varying degrees. Animal Nemesis: Is this to Spud. Jerkass to One: While he can be an inconvenience to others, he is only ever intentionally antagonistic to Spud. Mixed Animal Species Team: Occasionally works together with Bird, such as “Roley to the Rescue” when trying to rescue a baby bird that fell down a chimney.

characters.name: Humpty
characters.description: Gluttonous Pig: He’s always ready to eat. One-Track-Minded Hunger: His second defining trait after being grumpy. He’ll eat anything and everything left within his reach, up to and including Spud’s nose and Bob’s hammer. Perpetual Frowner: Always grumpy. According to Farmer Pickles, he was even named Humpty because “he always acts like he’s got a hump about him.” Sinister Swine: He’s may not have evil intents, but Humpty isalwaysangry, hence his name.

characters.name: Mr. Adams
characters.description: Arc Villian: Of "Bob's Big Plan". Unlike most examples, he doesn't act antagonistic towards Bob at any point in the special. One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Bob’s Big Plan”, theProject: Build Itera's pilot episode.

characters.name: Marjorie
characters.description: Early-Bird Cameo: She doesn’t properly debut untilProject: Build It, but she was offhandedly mentioned all the way back inSeason 2of the classic series.

characters.name: David Dailey
characters.description: Alliterative Name:DavidDailey. One-Shot Character: Aside from a random background cameo in “Scoop Knows It All”, he only appears in “Benny’s Important Job”.

characters.name: Sandy Beach
characters.description: Punny Name:Sunny Beach.

characters.name: Sunny and Saffron
characters.description: Alliterative Family: They alliterate with each other, although not with their parents. Meaningful Name: The connection between Sunny’s name and Sunflower Valley is obvious, while saffron is a shade of golden yellow commonly associated with sunflowers.

characters.name: Max
characters.description: Alliterative Family: Downplayed; he alliterates with his wife but not their two children. The Bus Came Back: After “Dizzy’s Sleepover”, he’s absent for a whole season before reappearing in “Roley’s Apple Press”.

characters.name: Mr. Battersby
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Dizzy’s Sleepover”. Steven Ulysses Perhero: He’s a chiropterologist named Mr.Battersby.

characters.name: The Pickers
characters.description: Rhyming Names: Well, their first names allalmostrhyme with “Picker”.

characters.name: Carlo and Cassia Sabatini
characters.description: Half-Identical Twins: And they’re even easier to tell apart because: Pink Girl, Blue Boy: Cassia always wears red and Carlo always wears blue.

characters.name: Ella Stevenson
characters.description: Two-Teacher School: Appears to be theonlyteacher at the school in Sunflower Valley.

characters.name: Annie Pickles
characters.description: Remember the New Guy?: She’s another example of what might be called a “suddenly-existent cousin”.

characters.name: MegMacDonald
characters.description: Alliterative Name:MegMacDonald Stock Foreign Name: She’s Scottish and her last name is”MacDonald”.

characters.name: Rio Rogers
characters.description: Alliterative Name:RioRogers

characters.name: Rusty Rogers
characters.description: Alliterative Name:RustyRogers. Meaningful Name: "Rusty" refers rather well to the color of the scenes he appears in (see below). Monochrome Past: The flashbacks he appears in are all extremely sepia-toned.

characters.name: Chip Chipper
characters.description: Repetitive Name: Duh. Steven Ulysses Perhero: He’s a woodsman whose name refers to woodchips… twice. You Don't Look Like You: When he returned to the show inReady, Steady, Build, the animators inexplicably gave him a mustache.

characters.name: Monty Mickelson
characters.description: Alliterative Name:MontyMicelson One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Scrambler To The Rescue”.

characters.name: Oscar Flicks
characters.description: Steven Ulysses Perhero: He’s a film director whose first name is a prestigious movie award, and whose last name is derived from a slang term for movies.

characters.name: Pablo Sabatini
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Packer’s Trailer Trouble”, aside from a random background cameo in “Race To The Finish”.

characters.name: Llewela
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Spud And The Hotel”.

characters.name: Mike Turntable
characters.description: Steven Ulysses Perhero: He’s a DJ whose last name refers to a common piece of DJ equipment.

characters.name: Mr. Carruthers
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Only appears in “An Inspector Calls”, the finale of theProject: Build Itera.

characters.name: Scrambler
characters.description: Catchphrase: Has a few.“Wicked!”“Let’s scram!”“Awesome!”“Sunflower Valley rocks!”“Jump on, we’ll be there in Scrambler time!” (Sometimes, “get there in Scrambler time!”)“Cool as a mule!”“This Scrambler's ready for action!” “Sunflower Valley rocks!” “Jump on, we’ll be there in Scrambler time!” (Sometimes, “get there in Scrambler time!”) “This Scrambler's ready for action!” Sixth Ranger: For Bob’s machine team.

characters.name: Sumsy
characters.description: Catchphrase: “I can pack ‘em, I can stack ‘em!” Reused Character Design: She uses a repainted version of Trix’s model but without the exhaust pipes.

characters.name: Packer
characters.description: Catchphrase: He has two.“Pack me up and watch me go-go!”“Fetch and deliver! Fetch and deliver!” “Pack me up and watch me go-go!” “Fetch and deliver! Fetch and deliver!” Image Song:Halfway Down the Hill

characters.name: Jackaroo
characters.description: Catchphrase: “Hot diggity dog!” and “Jumping jackrabbits!”

characters.name: R.Vee
characters.description: A Dog Named "Cat": He’s a cargo van,notan RV (Recreational Vehicle). One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Scrambler To The Rescue”.

characters.name: Dodger
characters.description: Catchphrase: “Dodger delivers!”

characters.name: Tumbler
characters.description: Bully Turned Buddy: He spends most of “Tumbler And The Skate Park” mocking Dizzy, but ends up apologizing and befriending her when, despite how he’d been treating her, she still turns up to help him when he gets lost. Catchphrase: “Rumbling and a-tumbling!” Singing in the Shower: The short “Tumbler Cleans Up” has him singing in the truck wash, much to the annoyance of Flex and Dodger.

characters.name: Flex
characters.description: Catchphrase: "Fantastic Flex!" and “Ducking down, swinging round, reaching for the sky, my clever flexable arm can go everywhere!” Meaningful Name: Named for hisflexible arm.

characters.name: Bristle
characters.description: Catchphrase: “Clean-As-A-Whistle Bristle, that's me!” (or sometimes just simply “Clean as a whistle!”) and “Spic and span!” Meaningful Name: Named for the bristles on his brushes.

characters.name: Splasher
characters.description: Amphibious Automobile: He is one. Catchphrase: “Never fear, Super Splasher is here!” Also, "Splasheriffic!" and "Splash-tastic!" Meaningful Name: Well, water splashes, doesn’t it?

characters.name: Gripper & Grabber
characters.description: Catchphrase: “Gripper and Grabber, Grabber and Gripper, having fun together!” The Dividual: They’re usually this, although “Race To The Finish” does have them briefly splitting up after an argument. Remember the New Guy?: They showed up this way by accident, as their first chronological appearance in the show happened in an episode that ended up airing a few months before their proper introduction in “Race To The Finish”.

characters.name: David Mockney
characters.description: Accidental Misnaming: He once accidentally calls Scoop “Scoot”. Dub Name Change: Becomes “Senyor Polsinós” (“Mr. Dusty”) in the show’s Catalan dub. Named After Somebody Famous: His name is a spoof on the British artist David Hockney.

characters.name: Brad Rad
characters.description: Rhyming Names: Yup, it rhymes. Surfer Dude: You betcha. Totally Radical: Even his name is bodacious, dude.

characters.name: Pirate Brickbeard
characters.description: Captain Colorbeard: Well, sort of- his beard the somewhat-dark-red color normally seen on bricks. Gold-Colored Superiority: That golden, jewel-encrusted hammer is absolutely useless for building anything, butmandoes it look cool. Real After All: At the end of the special, Bob and the crew do actually locate his old pirate ship- and the treasure chest containing the golden hammer.

characters.name: Carly
characters.description: Expy: The one episode she appeared in had her acting rather like Kelly fromHandy Manny, and helpfully handing out exactly the parts people need. (Except for a replacement trampoline spring- after Bob’s big order, she’s completely out.) Her name even sounds similar,especiallywhen spoken with a British accent (“Cahly”). One-Shot Character: Well, sheprobablywould’ve had more appearances if she hadn’t debuted in the last regular episode ofReady, Steady, Build.

characters.name: Tony Toberomi
characters.description: Alliterative Name:TonyToberomi.

characters.name: Scratch
characters.description: The Baby of the Bunch: He acts much younger than Bob’s other machines. In fact, he's technically younger than Dizzy. Cousin Oliver: Younger than everyone else? Check. Only debuted in a semi-reboot/spin-off of the show? Check. Image Song:“I’m A Digger With A Heart Of Gold”. He also joined Scoop and Muck in the US version of"Diggin' It Up". Suspiciously Similar Substitute: He acts very much like (a slightly more annoying version of) Benny.

characters.name: Rubble
characters.description: Compressed Vice: He’s extremely impatient and constantly rushes to get jobs done in “Rubble And The Seagull Surprise”, and never acts like that in any other episode whatsoever. Luck-Based Search Technique: At the end of “Rubble And The Seagull Surprise”, he rolls over an odd rock poking out of the ground and discovers that it’s actually a fossil pterodactyl wing. Meaningful Name: Dump trucks are meant for hauling dirt and, well, rubble.

characters.name: Leo
characters.description: Missing Mom: While his father, Curtis, is a semi-regular feature of the show, his mother hasn’t made a single appearance. Obsessive Hobby Episode:“Spring City TV”has him become addicted to playingFurious Fishon his phone, much to the detriment of his work. Token Minority: He’s the only member of the main cast to be Black, although as the main cast only has three humans in it the ethnicity ratio isn’ttoobad.

characters.name: Mayor Madison
characters.description: Alliterative Name:MariaMadison. Her title, “Mayor”, adds another level of alliteration. One-Hour Work Week: Her mayoring seems to mostly consist of A. photo-ops and speeches, and B. calling Bob every so often with a new public works project she wants him to build. If an episode is built around her doing the latter, she’s pretty much never too busy to stop by Bob and check out what he’s doing, although unspecified “mayor duties” do occasionally get used as aDeus ex 'Scuse Meto keep her from hanging around for too long.

characters.name: Chef Seymour Tattie
characters.description: Gratuitous Foreign Language: The standard thing he does upon entering a scene is to say “hello” in two or three languages. “Hola! Konichiwa! Guten Tag!” New Job as the Plot Demands: While most chefs would stick to working at just one restaurant, he doesn’t; he owns the Flying Saucepan, a milkshake bar, the Play Cafe, and an unnamed restaurant Bob built in “Machine Magic”. How he manages to keep all of these places running at once is a bit of a mystery, especially since the audience never sees him having any sort of staff running the places while he’s gone.

characters.name: The Spring City Rockets
characters.description: Free-Range Children: While sometimes they do have Betsy with them to provide adult supervision, they’ve still been seen by themselves a lot more than they really ought to be. Kid-Appeal Character: Their main purpose for existing in the show. Retro Rocket: A teal-colored one is their group logo. Shouldn't We Be in School Right Now?: They always seem to be out and about in Spring City regardless of the time of day, despite the fact that theyhavebeen established as being students of Spring City Elementary. Sucks at Dancing: Brandon is shown to have this problem in “Ballroom Blitz”, although he still participates in the group’s street dance act for camaraderie reasons.

characters.name: Jenny Dobbs
characters.description: Last Place You Look: When a long-haired Peruvian guinea pig disappears in “Lofty Lets Loose”, where does it turn out to be? Why, clinging on to her back. Noodle Incident: When Wendy tells her that there might be “one place [she] didn’t look” for a lost guinea pig, she begs not to have to go back into the monkey house. Why, exactly, she doesn’t want to go in there is never explained.

characters.name: Tilly Corner
characters.description: Birds of a Feather: It’s only fitting that she, a vet, would end up marrying Henry Corner, who works with horses for a living. Limited Wardrobe: She always wears her blue-green veterinary uniform, even outside of work. And even in “Wild Wild Wedding”, when you’d expect her to be dressed formally for her marriage with Henry.

characters.name: Curtis
characters.description: Birthday Episode: His birthday happens in “Workshop Makeover”.

characters.name: Skye Sampson
characters.description: Alliterative Name:SkyeSampson

characters.name: Sylvan de Souza
characters.description: Alliterative Name:Sylvan deSouza. Impossibly Awesome Magic Trick: While most of the tricks he pulls off in “Machine Magic” are within reason, vanishing into a cloud of purple smoke and sparkles that appeared out of nowhere isnot. New Job as the Plot Demands: He goes from being a dancer in “Ballroom Blitz” to being a stage magician in “Machine Magic”. Non-Specifically Foreign: His first name is French in origin, his last name is Portuguese, and his accent is…all over the place. Show Within a Show: He’s the star of a dancing-based TV show calledBallroom Blitz, which was introduced in the episode of the same name.

characters.name: Henry Corner
characters.description: Birds of a Feather: Ends up marrying Tilly, who also works with animals.

characters.name: Carl Parker
characters.description: Show Within a Show: He’s responsible for directing a few, including the famous (at least in-universe)Space Robots 3and all of Dash Lightning’s flicks.

characters.name: Dash Lightning
characters.description: Awesome McCoolname:Dash Lightning. Catchphrase: All of his movies have him saying “With a zoom! Boom! Lightning… strikes twice!” at at least one point. Early-Bird Cameo: He doesn’t properly debut until “Star Struck Muck”, but the giant TV screen from “Rockets Under The Stars” briefly plays a scene from one of his movies when Bob tests it. The Knights Who Say "Squee!": As it turns out, hereallyloves anything to do about building, so he squees over Bob’s gang about as much as they squee over him. Show Within a Show: He’s starred in such classics asCretaceous Park: Megadino AttackandShark 2: The Return Of Bruce.

characters.name: Mei Moon
characters.description: Alliterative Name:MeiMoon. Birthday Episode: “A Message From Space” takes place on the birthday of her and her twin sister Lin. Labcoat of Science and Medicine: She wears one, despite astronomy not really being the sort of scientific discipline to require a labcoat. Omnidisciplinary Scientist: She works at an observatory, so sheshouldbe an astronomer… except her debut episode has her launching a rocket into space (rocketetry has more in common withengineeringthan astronomy), and “Jet Powered Scoop” has Bob build her a scientific test facility that has little, if anything, to do with outer space. Steven Ulysses Perhero: She’s a space scientist with the surname “Moon”.

characters.name: Lin Moon
characters.description: Always Identical Twins: In this caseliterallyidentical, as the animators just copy-pasted Mei’s character model to make her. Including Mei’sLabcoat of Science and Medicine, although they tried to hide that by only showing her head. Birthday Episode: She and Mei have a birthday video chat in “A Message From Space”. Gender-Blender Name: While the writers of the show did know that “Lin” was a genuine Chinese name, they failed to realize that it was amalename. Oopsie. One-Shot Character: Only appears on Mei’s video screen in “A Message From Space”. Remember the New Guy?: While her not physically appearing before on the show is excusable due to, you know, beingin space, that doesn’t explain why Mei never brought her up to anyone before the events of “A Message From Space”.

characters.name: Conrad
characters.description: Big Bad: OfMega Machines: The Movieand, for that matter, the first actual villain in the series. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: He spends most ofMega Machinespretending to be nice, helpful, and not at all angry that Bob got the building contract over him, when, in reality, he controls his Mega Machine crew with yelling and threats of the the scrap heap, and he sabotages the dam- risking the flooding and destruction of Spring City- just to get back at Bob. Fat Bastard: He’s the first villain of the show, and he has a noticeable gut. "Metaphor" Is My Middle Name: While trying to ingratiate himself to Bob he says thatConrad: “Helpful” is my middle name.Muck:That’s a funny name. Vile Villain, Saccharine Show: For a children's series featuring live machines hereallystands out as being the vilest, as he started a flood so he could ruin Bob's good name andthreatens to scrap his machines if they don't do his bidding.

characters.name: Roland
characters.description: New Job as the Plot Demands: He works at a warehouse, directing the destinations of shipping containers… but he also owns a grocery store in “Ace’s Dynamite Movie”. Poor Communication Kills: In “The Dash Lightning Experience”, he tells Muck that the props for the titular ride are in the container “on the end”, and fails to specify which end. Of course, this leads to Muck and Lofty taking the wrong container.

characters.name: Emily Twain
characters.description: Fictional Document: She’s written a few of these- specifically, a series of mystery novels starring a squad of detectives. In “Bob’s Detective Squad”, Mayor Madison has Rocky keep a copy of her latest book safe, only for it to go missing- time for him to play detective. One-Shot Character: Only appears in “Bob’s Detective Squad”. Named After Somebody Famous: Her surname obviously referencesMark Twain.

characters.name: Tiny
characters.description: Advertised Extra: He only appears in a handful of episodes, and half the times when hedoesappear it’s mostly as part of a building montage with few or no spoken lines. Somehow, though, not only does he get featured in the background of the show’s intro sequence, he also gets his own “Meet The Team” short onYouTube-afterhe’d already taken over half of Lofty’s! The Big Guy: He's tallest machine of Bob's crew, even after Ace, Crunch, and Thud joined them. Catchphrase: “Time for some Tower Power!” Dub Name Change: Becomes “Winzi” in the German dub. Ironic Name: He’s named Tiny, but he’s the biggest machine on Bob’s crew (at least until Ace, Crunch, and Thud joined up at the end ofMega Machines: The Movie, but even then, he's still the tallest machine).

characters.name: Two-Tonne
characters.description: Alliterative Name:Two-Tonne Dub Name Change: Becomes “Schleppo” in the German dub. Meaningful Name: A “two-ton truck” is an actual thing (the name derives from the amount of weight they can carry), although Two-Tonne has been shown carrying loads that really ought to weigh more than just two tons. Out of Focus: Despite being part of the main cast (even having a part in the intro where gets name dropped, especially in Season 3), he barely gets enough screen time with the team. In fact, he is the only vehicle not to have any episodes dedicated to him. Even Tiny, Ace, Crunch, and Thud as well asDemoted to Extracharacters, Dizzy and Roley, had at least one dedicated episode for them. Why Did It Have to Be Snakes?: In “A Heavy Load”, he’s revealed to be afraid of elephants- which is not fun when the zoo needs one transported. He ends up getting over his fear by the end of the episode.

characters.name: Betsy
characters.description: Dub Name Change: She’s “Betti” in the German dub. Team Mom: Serves as this for the Spring City Rockets.

characters.name: Phillip
characters.description: Break the Haughty: In “Phillip’s Important Job”, after he insults Betsy by telling her that he’s more important than she is, he then runs out of fuel on the side of the road (due to dashing off to pick up Mayor Madison before Curtis had a chance to fill his tank), and then needs to be towed back to Curtis’ workshop due to the possibility that running on fumes for a bit damaged his engine. And who ends up taking Mayor Madison to where she needed to go that day? Why, Betsy!

characters.name: Alfred
characters.description: Aerith and Bob: While most of the machines in the show have names derived from their functions or appearance, he’s just named “Alfred”.

characters.name: Stretch
characters.description: Dub Name Change: Becomes “Schaufli” in the German dub.

characters.name: Tread
characters.description: Dub Name Change: He’s “Hubi” in the German dub.

characters.name: Shifter
characters.description: Dub Name Change: Becomes “Türmer” in the German dub. Meaningful Name: Named for a forklift’s ability to shift materials around the work area. Oblivious Guilt Slinging: He spends a scene or two in “Shifter’s New Garage” talking about how excited he is to get a new garage to Dizzy- who is there to tell him that he’s not actually going to have one.

characters.name: Ace
characters.description: Absurd Phobia: For some reason, he’s afraid of bees. Which are A. tiny, and B. unable to sting him because he’s a machine. Oh, and this fear ended up leading to him losing his TV show. Catchphrase: “Let’s do this!” As he used to be the star of his own TV show, this being his catchphrase is acknowledged in-universe. Image Song:"Feel the Force" Noodle Incident: He mentions a few of these inn “Scoop’s Big Oops”:He once accidentally dropped a load of scrap iron on his old boss. (This isn’t to be confused with the incident that ended his TV career, which can be read underResolved Noodle Incident.)Once, he managed to inadvertently dig up buried treasure. Pirates were involved. (This story actually gets told so the audience can hear at the end of the episode- except the credits start while Ace is still just setting the scene.) He once accidentally dropped a load of scrap iron on his old boss. (This isn’t to be confused with the incident that ended his TV career, which can be read underResolved Noodle Incident.)

characters.name: Crunch
characters.description: The Dog Bites Back: After being shouted at and threatened with being sent to the scrap heap by Conrad for most ofMega Machines, he and Thud finally turn on him once his scheme is revealed, although, withBob the Builderbeing the kind of show it is, the only thing they get to do is menacingly advance a few steps towards him before Mayor Madison picks up the situation. Intelligible Unintelligible: In “Lofty’s Playground Problem”, Thud is somehow able to understand his grunting perfectly. The Unintelligible: While his appearance in Season 3 would upgrade him into anIntelligible Unintelligible, inMega Machinesabsolutelyno onecould understand his grunting- not Conrad, not Ace, and certainly not any members of Bob’s crew.

characters.name: Thud
characters.description: The Dog Bites Back: After being shouted at and threatened with being sent to the scrap heap by Conrad for most ofMega Machines, he and Crunch finally turn on him once his scheme is revealed, although, withBob the Builderbeing the kind of show it is, the only thing they get to do is menacingly advance a few steps towards him before Mayor Madison picks up the situation.

characters.name: Rocky
characters.description: Dub Name Change: Becomes “Packie” in the German dub. Fanboy: In “Bob’s Detective Squad”, he’s revealed to be abiiitof a fan of author Emily Twain, and getsslightlyoverexcited when he finally meets her.

characters.name: Picksy
characters.description: Barely Changed Dub Name: Is called “Picksi” in the German dub. A Lizard Named "Liz": A cherry-picker named Picksy. Pink Girl, Blue Boy: She’s reddish-pink, while Lofty, who… um, hasCannot Talk to Womenissues with her specifically, is blue. Satellite Love Interest: Well, sort of. To explicitly spell out the situation between her and Lofty: Lofty, after meeting her in “Talking Loud And Clear”, finds himself struggling to talk to her without stammering for unstated reasons. This is usually used in fiction as an indication of a crush, but when he finally does manage to talk to her properly, he only says he wanted them to be “friends”. So who knows what’s going on there. But shedefinitelyfits the “lady with minimal signs of a personality defined mostly by how a male character acts around her” part of this trope.

characters.name: Norm
characters.description: Early-Bird Cameo: He isn’t actually properly introduced until “Tunnel Trouble”, but he can be briefly seen working on Mei’s underground test facility in “Jet Powered Scoop”. One-Word Vocabulary: Well, two-word vocabulary.Norm: Dig. Dig. Tunnel. Tunnel. Punny Name: He’s enormous.

characters.name: Gull
characters.description: A Dog Named "Dog": A seagull named Gull. Snack-Stealing Seagulls: Snatches a sushi roll out of Bob’s hand in “Machine Magic”. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: To Bird from the original series. Unwitting Instigator of Doom: Becomes this when he perches on the rim of Mei’s radio telescope in “A Message Of Space”, as Lofty accidentally wrecks the thing while trying to get him to fly off- ironically, because he was worried Gull would damage it.

characters.name: Whizzer
characters.description: Cute Machines: Oh, so much. He’s even able to cute Muck into taking him out to a build despite Wendy having told Muck not to do that exact thing. Fast Tunneling: That little drill on his front somehow allows him to move through the ground as fast as he dashes around on top of it, despite the amount of material he’d need to move in order to do that. Grappling-Hook Pistol: He has a grappling hook (and all the dubious physics that comes with it) built into him, as if his tunneling ability didn’t make him hard enough to catch already. Meaningful Name: His name is derived either from his speed or from the sound his motors make. Red Eyes, Take Warning: Subverted in “Talking Loud And Clear”- Lofty sees that his eyes are lit up red and assumes that it must mean something bad, but it turns out that they just glow red when he’s charging (and, presumably, turn green once his battery fills up).
